title: OSMA set to return with new restaurant and grocery in the city centre
summary: From Prestwich to the city centre, it's a new chapter for Dani Heron and Sofie Stoermann-Naess Go to Manchester's Finest X profile OSMA is set to return to Manchester this autumn, with founders Danielle Heron and Sofie Stoermann-Naess bringing the restaurant back in a new city ce
lead: From Prestwich to the city centre, it's a new chapter for Dani Heron and Sofie Stoermann-Naess Go to Manchester's Finest X profile OSMA is set to return to Manchester this autumn, with founders Danielle Heron and Sofie Stoermann-Naess bringing the restaurant back in a new city ce
practical_angle: Новое место или запуск стоит проверить перед визитом.
raw_evidence: From Prestwich to the city centre, it's a new chapter for Dani Heron and Sofie Stoermann-Naess Go to Manchester's Finest X profile OSMA is set to return to Manchester this autumn, with founders Danielle Heron and Sofie Stoermann-Naess bringing the restaurant back in a new city centre location alongside an ambitious new grocery concept The restaurant will reopen at One Port Street in the Northern Quarter, almost two years after closing its original Prestwich site. The move marks a new chapter for the pair, who will also launch Boutikk, a neighbouring provisions store inspired by Scandinavian food culture and tr Originally opened in 2020, OSMA quickly established itself as one of Greater Manchester’s most acclaimed neighbourhood restaurants. Named after a combination of Oslo and Manchester, the restaurant blended Stoermann-Naess’ Norwegian heritage with Heron’s classical culinary trainin The return will see Heron resume her role as Executive Chef, with a menu centred around seasonal produce, fresh seafood, local meats and vegetable-led dishes. A number of OSMA’s most popular plates are expected to make a comeback, alongside the restaurant’s well-known sharing Sun While the restaurant occupies one side of the development, Boutikk will introduce a new offering to the city centre. The grocery and provisions shop will stock seasonal fruit and vegetables, dairy products, fresh meat and fish, with chefs available to advise customers on ingredie

fact_card.event_name: OSMA set to return with new restaurant and grocery in the city centre

raw_evidence: Just four months after announcing plans to close, the Urmston restaurant is relaunching with a new sharing plates concept Go to Manchester's Finest X profile Back in February, it looked like the end for Örme The Michelin Guide-listed neighbourhood restaurant in Urmston announced plans to sell the business and close its doors, citing the ‘significant economic pressures’ facing the hospitality industry and describing long-term sustainability as ‘unfeasible’ Now, in a surprise turnaround, owners have confirmed it will remain open and relaunch on 1 July with a completely new concept centred around sharing plates, social dining and a more relaxed approach to eating out The restaurant, which opened in 2023 and quickly earned recognition from both the Michelin Guide and Good Food Guide, is moving away from its tasting menu format in favour of a regularly changing menu of snacks, small plates and desserts designed to be shared around the table Dishes set to feature on the opening menu include salt’n’vinegar scampi with curry mayo, smoked Lancashire cheese custard on fried bread with red onion jam, cured Cornish mackerel with roasted onion dressing and English peas, and spring lamb served with sweet and sour mint and BB

Since they started in 2020, Eat Well MCR is a lifeline to people across the region By Manchester's Finest, Last updated 17 June 2026 Go to Manchester's Finest X profile Since it started in the days after lockdown, Eat Well MCR has delivered more than 150,000 meals to people who need them across Greater Manchester – all cooked by some of the city’s most prestigious restaurants Recruiting the like of Dishoom , Hawksmoor , Hello Oriental and Maray , among many, many others (too many to name check here), they give back to the city without fanfare and for all the right reasons We spent a day out on the road with some of the team, to see first-hand some of the vital work they do – from the kitchens making up the meals all the way through to the people who receive them The whole thing is magnificent; a testament both to the tenacity of its founders, Kathleen O’Connor, Gemma Saunders and Mary-Ellen McTague, and also to the city’s chefs who quietly but diligently support what has become an essential lifeline for so many

Featuring 300 seats and designed for all travellers, the lounge combines award-winning service, thoughtfully designed surroundings, and e “We’re delighted to announce our third lounge at Manchester Airport and second in Terminal 2. summary: A tiny new coffee shop has opened in Ancoats, and it’s already turning heads with a simple mission – making quality coffee affordable again 7ZZ (pronounced ‘seven zeez’) has quietly launched on Oldham Road in a space no bigger than your arm span Owner Joe Stephens is bringing spe

practical_angle: Новое место или запуск стоит проверить перед визитом.
raw_evidence: A tiny new coffee shop has opened in Ancoats, and it’s already turning heads with a simple mission – making quality coffee affordable again 7ZZ (pronounced ‘seven zeez’) has quietly launched on Oldham Road in a space no bigger than your arm span Owner Joe Stephens is bringing speciality coffee prices back down to earth with flat whites starting from just £2.50 and matcha from only £4 In a city where a morning coffee can easily set you back more than a fiver, the new independent hopes to bridge the gap between premium coffee shops and budget chains The compact café may be easy to miss at first glance, but its menu packs plenty of personality. Alongside classic espresso-based drinks, customers can pick up ceremonial-grade matcha, iced barista-made coffees, and vibrant ube lattes made properly (no purple syrup in sight) Joe says the idea behind 7ZZ was to create a space where customers don’t have to choose between quality and affordability, with a price list that rivals Greggs

title: Manchester cocktail bar added to Michelin Guide in ‘incredible surprise'
summary: A cocktail bar in Manchester has received a surprise listing in the prestigious Michelin Guide – the first drinks-led venue in the city to gain such recognition Posie opened less than a year ago in an unlikely location just off Market Street, transforming a former sandwich shop i
lead: A cocktail bar in Manchester has received a surprise listing in the prestigious Michelin Guide – the first drinks-led venue in the city to gain such recognition Posie opened less than a year ago in an unlikely location just off Market Street, transforming a former sandwich shop i
practical_angle: Новое место или запуск стоит проверить перед визитом.
raw_evidence: A cocktail bar in Manchester has received a surprise listing in the prestigious Michelin Guide – the first drinks-led venue in the city to gain such recognition Posie opened less than a year ago in an unlikely location just off Market Street, transforming a former sandwich shop into a sunny and stylish bar, right in the heart of Manchester city centre The bar comes from the same team behind 10 Tib Lane (which is also a Michelin Guide venue) and is famed for its cocktails, oysters, and global wine list And now Posie has swooped into the prestigious guide just 10 months after its launch, with judges celebrating its small plates and its extensive cocktail list The Michelin Guide said: “Founded by the same team as nearby 10 Tib Lane, posie is a little more drink-driven than its sibling, with a global wine selection that’s keen on natural options, alongside over 30 classic and bespoke cocktails and mocktails “None of this means that they don’t take their food seriously, with plenty of flavour bursting out of the concise small-plates selection

fact_card.event_name: Manchester cocktail bar added to Michelin Guide in ‘incredible surprise'

raw_evidence: The Manchester restaurant is opening up its mezzanine kitchen to a new independent food concept Eating & Drinking ⸱ Food Halls and Markets ⸱ Street Food Go to Manchester's Finest X profile Hello Oriental is searching for a new East Asian food operator to take over the mezzanine level at its Circle Square venue The restaurant, bar and events space, located just off Oxford Road, is looking for an independent chef, pop-up, street food trader or established brand to join the site as it develops what the team believes will be Manchester’s first multi-vendor destination dedicated to East Asi Unlike a traditional food hall, the plan is to keep the focus on East Asian cooking, with Hello Oriental looking for a concept that complements its existing food and drink offer The team is particularly interested in ramen, noodles, dumplings, Korean street food, Japanese comfort food, Taiwanese dishes, Hong Kong-style food, barbecue, izakaya-style small plates or modern East Asian fusion

title: Porta are taking over the grills at Open Fire Dining Club for a Spanish feast built around giant paella
summary: Porta and Tartuffe have spent months building a 10 course Spanish-inspired menu of Gildas, grilled seafood, chicken thighs and giant open-fire paella By Manchester's Finest, Last updated 9 July 2026 Go to Manchester's Finest X profile There are few restaurants that inspire the ki
lead: Porta and Tartuffe have spent months building a 10 course Spanish-inspired menu of Gildas, grilled seafood, chicken thighs and giant open-fire paella By Manchester's Finest, Last updated 9 July 2026 Go to Manchester's Finest X profile There are few restaurants that inspire the ki
practical_angle: Новое место или запуск стоит проверить перед визитом.
raw_evidence: Porta and Tartuffe have spent months building a 10 course Spanish-inspired menu of Gildas, grilled seafood, chicken thighs and giant open-fire paella By Manchester's Finest, Last updated 9 July 2026 Go to Manchester's Finest X profile There are few restaurants that inspire the kind of loyalty Porta does. The sort of place where you go in for a vermouth and a couple of plates, then realise two hours later that you’ve ordered half the menu and are seriously considering another That easy-going, no-fuss approach has made the walk-in-only tapas bar a favourite across its four sites in Chester, Altrincham, Salford and West Didsbury. Founded by brothers Ben and Joe Wright, Porta takes its cues from the neighbourhood bars of Spain: short menus, good produce, For the next Open Fire Dining Club , that Spanish influence is being taken out of Porta’s dining rooms and into the open air at Torr Vale Mill. Across the food and drink, the afternoon will move around the western Mediterranean – from Gildas, grilled seafood and giant paella to t On 18 and 19 July , Porta joins resident chefs Tartuffe for a collaboration that has been in the works for months

title: Gaucho Manchester unveils major refurbishment ahead of August reopening - About Manchester
summary: Gaucho Manchester begins an exciting new chapter as the restaurant unveils its newly redesigned interior in the heart of the city Marking more than 25 years in Manchester, thereopening celebrates the next evolution of the brand in a city that has been central to its story The new

raw_evidence: Gaucho Manchester begins an exciting new chapter as the restaurant unveils its newly redesigned interior in the heart of the city Marking more than 25 years in Manchester, thereopening celebrates the next evolution of the brand in a city that has been central to its story The newly transformed 205-cover space introduces a warm, contemporary design direction inspired by the landscapes, textures and spirit of Latin America. A timeless palette of earthy tones is layered with natural timber, rich marble, atmospheric lighting and refined finishes,creat Housed within one of Manchester’s most distinctive 19th-century former churches, the new design complements the original character of the building Restored architectural features,including the original church organ, remain central to the space while a mezzanine dining level offers elevated views across the restaurant below At the centre of the restaurant, a renewed open kitchen brings the theatre of the grill into view, creating a closer connection between guests and the craft behind every dish

fact_card.event_name: Gaucho Manchester unveils major refurbishment ahead of August reopening - About Manchester

title: Rudy's to open in Monton Salford - About Manchester
summary: Rudy’s Pizza Napoletana has confirmed it will be bringing its relaxed dining experience and renowned Neapolitan pizzas to Salford for the first time, with a new restaurant opening in Monton village this autumn. To celebrate, the pizzeria is giving away 3,000 pizzas on the house a
lead: Rudy’s Pizza Napoletana has confirmed it will be bringing its relaxed dining experience and renowned Neapolitan pizzas to Salford for the first time, with a new restaurant opening in Monton village this autumn.
practical_angle: Новое место или запуск стоит проверить перед визитом.
raw_evidence: Rudy’s Pizza Napoletana has confirmed it will be bringing its relaxed dining experience and renowned Neapolitan pizzas to Salford for the first time, with a new restaurant opening in Monton village this autumn. To celebrate, the pizzeria is giving away 3,000 pizzas on the house a The new pizzeria, located at 237-239 Monton Road , will transform the former Bennetts Furnishers store that has been vacant since 2022, with work now officially underway. The spacious venue will mark Rudy’s 41st restaurant to date Since launching in Manchester’s Ancoats in 2015, Rudy’s has grown into one of the UK’s most loved pizzerias, celebrated for its light, double fermented dough and unwavering dedication to true Neopolitan craft Testament to its authenticity, Rudy’s is the largest organisation in the world to be accredited by the Associazione Verace Pizza Napoletana (AVPN), the international body that protects and promotes traditional Neapolitan pizza making Rudy’s has also been recognised among the World’s 50 Best Pizzeria, ranking 23rd globally for 2025. Each pizza, from the classic Margherita to the fiery Calabrese, is made to AVPN standards using traditional techniques and ingredients sourced directly from Italy To claim one of 3,000 complimentary pizzas, Monton locals can sign up via this link , where they will be able to choose from Rudy’s favourites such as its Cinghiale , Margherita Con Bufala, and Triple Pepperoni to enjoy at the new opening this summer

fact_card.event_name: Rudy's to open in Monton Salford - About Manchester

raw_evidence: Manchester Airport has welcomed its first-ever Sainsbury’s store, which opened its doors this morning The Sainsbury’s Local, located in T3 Arrivals, makes Manchester the third UK airport to host a Sainsbury’s store – joining Edinburgh Airport, where Sainsbury’s opened its first airport store in 2024, and London Luton The store, which is expected to create 24 jobs, will offer a carefully curated selection of Sainsbury’s great value, high-quality convenience products, from meal deals and food on the go to a wide range of home and kitchen essentials The new store will also allow customers to order online from Sainsbury’s much-loved Argos and Habitat brands for collection, providing easy access to thousands of products Terminal 3 is currently undergoing a multi-million pound revamp, with a new 400+-seat sports bar opening earlier this year in the Departure lounge and an Italian eatery, Terracotta, set to follow later this year. The terminal has increased in size following the closure of Termina Richard Jackson, Retail Director at Manchester Airport, said
